steps.title: Use present tense for facts
steps.content: The sentence states a permanent scientific fact: a helium atom has 2 protons. General facts and defining properties are normally expressed in the simple present tense.

steps.title: Reject tense mismatches
steps.content: Choice A implies the atom acquired the property at some point, Choice C suggests a temporary ongoing action, and Choice D incorrectly places the fact only in the past. The simple present form states the permanent property. The answer is B.
